title: Articles in fixed expressions
lesson: Many fixed expressions settle the article for you. Some drop it entirely (go to bed, at home, by car, at night); others always keep 'the' (in the morning, play the piano). Learn these as whole phrases rather than applying the usual rules.

items.text: We go to {1} school by {2} bus.
items.explanation: 'Go to school' (as a student) and 'by bus' (means of transport) are fixed expressions with no article.

items.text: She stays at {1} home when she doesn't feel well.
items.explanation: 'At home' is a fixed expression with no article — contrast 'at the office', where a specific place is meant.

items.text: He plays {1} piano every night, skips {2} dinner, and then listens to {3} radio.
items.explanation: Musical instruments take 'the' (the piano); meals take no article (dinner); 'the radio' takes 'the' — unlike 'watch television' which takes no article.

items.text: They went to {1} bed early because they had {2} flu.
items.explanation: 'Go to bed' takes no article, but the illness 'the flu' takes 'the' — compare 'have a cold' which uses 'a'.

items.text: We usually have {1} lunch at one o'clock.
items.explanation: Meals take no article: have breakfast, have lunch, have dinner.
